title: The 3-Lens Perspective Shift
description: A conflict resolution tool based on narrative therapy, used when a client is heavily entrenched in being 'right'.

title: The Clarity-Capacity-Commitment Matrix
description: Used in the very first session to locate why a client has repeatedly failed to reach their goals in the past.

title: The Cognitive Restructuring Loop
description: A profound tool for dismantling limiting beliefs by separating emotional meaning from neutral facts.

title: The Friction-to-Flow Audit
description: Instead of adding willpower, this framework removes environmental friction to build unbreakable habits.

title: The Identity-Bridging Exercise
description: Behavior change fails when it opposes identity. This framework bridges 'who I am' with 'what I want to do.'

title: The Minimum Viable Boundary (MVB)
description: A step-by-step guide to helping chronic people-pleasers set boundaries incrementally without saying 'no'.

title: The Outcome-Obstacle-Opportunity (O3) Framework
description: A rapid-intervention model for fast-paced sessions where a client feels deeply stuck and overwhelmed.

title: The Quiet Authority Discovery Flow
description: An enrollment framework for coaches who feel gross selling, relying on deep listening and mutual assessment.

title: The Root-Cause Radar
description: An evolution of 'The 5 Whys' designed specifically for unpacking deeply held limiting beliefs without traumatizing the client.

title: The Zone of Alignment Audit
description: Helps coaches or entrepreneurial clients discover their actual profitable niche, not just the one that makes money.

title: The Difference Between Coaching, Consulting, and Therapy
description: Blurring these lines is the fastest way to ruin a client relationship, burn yourself out, or face ethical issues. Let's make this crystal clear.

title: The Honesty Guide to Executive Coaching
description: Executive coaching is often seen as the "holy grail" of the industry because of the high price tags. But before you pivot your brand, let's talk about the realities.

title: How to Fire a Coaching Client (Kindly)
description: Not every client is a good fit. Sometimes they consistently disrespect your boundaries. You can, and should, fire clients when the relationship becomes toxic.

title: How to Get Your First Coaching Client (Without Being Salesy)
description: The hardest client you will ever get is your first one. Here is the truth: your first client will almost never come from a clever Instagram funnel.

title: Imposter Syndrome is a Feature, Not a Bug
description: If you feel like an imposter right now, take a deep breath. It means you are doing it right. Why imposter syndrome is a sign of high standards.

title: Marketing for Coaches Who Hate Marketing
description: You became a coach to help people, not to perform trending dances on TikTok. How to grow your practice through Radical Generosity.

title: The Myth of the 'Six-Figure Coaching Business'
description: Scroll through your social feed right now, and you will see a dozen ads from coaches promising to teach you how to build a six-figure empire. It is a myth.

title: A Simple Offboarding Ritual to Prevent Buyer's Remorse
description: The last session is just as important as the first session. How a client feels when they leave dictates whether they will ever refer you.

title: Why Coaches Should Stop Charging by the Hour
description: If you are currently charging $100 for a 60-minute session, you are building a trap for yourself. Here is how to transition to value-based pricing.

title: What to Do When a Client Says "I Don't Know"
description: It is the four-word phrase that sends every new coach into a minor panic. Most coaches immediately try to rescue the client. Don't do that. Do this.

title: Start Here
description: A guided path for coaches at different stages of their journey.

title: Templates & Downloads
description: The complete toolkit for coaches. Open, editable, and free.

title: Annotations & Books
description: Curated reading skipping the fluffy hustle culture. Psychology, methodology, and ethics.

title: The Mission / About
description: Why we exist and what we believe about the coaching profession.
